meta_title: Impact Doors in Wyndham, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Wyndham, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, financing from $87/mo. Protect your Wyndham Estates home today.
h1: Impact Doors for Wyndham, FL Homes — Built Tough for Broward County Storms
intro:
Wyndham is one of Broward County's most desirable residential communities, and homeowners in Wyndham Estates have invested significantly in their properties — with average home values hovering around $480,000, protecting that investment is not just smart, it's essential. Located approximately 12 miles from the coast in the 33076 ZIP code, Wyndham may not sit on the shoreline, but that distance offers little comfort when a major hurricane pushes inland. The fierce wind fields that accompany Category 2, 3, and 4 storms extend dozens of miles from the eye, and Broward County's 140 mph wind zone designation reflects exactly how serious the threat is for neighborhoods like yours.

The entry points of your home — your front door, sliding glass doors, and French doors — are among the most structurally vulnerable elements during a storm. Standard doors, even those that appear solid and well-built, are not engineered to withstand the sustained wind speeds and wind-borne debris that Florida storms routinely produce. A door failure during a hurricane doesn't just mean a broken entryway; it can lead to catastrophic internal pressurization, roof uplift, and widespread structural damage throughout the entire home. For Wyndham Estates residents with beautifully appointed homes, the cost of that kind of damage can be devastating.

That's precisely why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — The Kings of Service — has become the trusted name for impact door installation throughout Wyndham and the greater Broward County area. Our team installs impact-resistant entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors engineered to meet and exceed Florida Building Code requirements for high-wind areas. Whether your home was built before 1994 and has never been upgraded, or you're updating a newer property with superior protection, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing delivers the expertise, product quality, and service that Wyndham homeowners deserve.

section_why.h2: Why Wyndham Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Door Installations
section_why.body:
When you're protecting a home worth nearly half a million dollars, the company you choose to install your impact doors matters enormously. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings over 60 years of combined installation experience to every project, and unlike many contractors in the South Florida market, we handle every aspect of the installation in-house. That means no subcontractors, no handoffs, and no gaps in accountability. The same skilled, licensed professionals who measure your openings are the ones who fabricate the framing, hang the doors, and ensure every seal and multi-point locking system is perfectly set.

Our team has completed impact door and window installations in more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, giving us deep familiarity with the specific construction styles, building materials, and structural nuances common in Broward County communities. Wyndham homes often feature stucco exteriors, concrete block construction, and larger architectural openings that require precise measurements and custom-fitted installations. We understand these details because we've worked in neighborhoods just like Wyndham Estates for years, and we bring that local knowledge to every project we take on in the 33076 area.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing holds an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau, and we maintain full licensing and insurance coverage as required by the state of Florida. We coordinate directly with the Broward County Building Division to pull all necessary permits and schedule required inspections, so you never have to navigate that process alone. Our commitment to doing things the right way — every time — is what earned us the title of The Kings of Service, and it's what keeps Wyndham homeowners referring us to their neighbors throughout Wyndham Estates and beyond.

We also understand that a home improvement project of this magnitude requires clear communication and transparency. From the moment we provide your free estimate to the day we complete your final walkthrough, our team keeps you informed, on schedule, and confident that your investment is in the right hands. That's the Premier Impact Windows & Roofing difference.

section_history.h2: Wyndham's Storm History Makes the Case for Impact Doors
section_history.body:
Broward County has experienced 8 significant storm events since 1990, and the damage patterns from those storms tell a clear story about why impact-rated doors are not optional for Wyndham homeowners. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was the storm that rewrote Florida's building codes and reshaped how the entire industry approached hurricane protection. While Andrew's direct path struck further south in Miami-Dade, its wind field was massive, and the structural failures it exposed — particularly in entry doors and garage doors — were widespread throughout Broward County communities. The post-Andrew reckoning led directly to the stricter Florida Building Code standards that govern construction and renovation in Wyndham today.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 was perhaps the most impactful storm for Broward County communities in recent memory. Wilma crossed the peninsula with sustained winds that battered inland areas for hours, and the damage to properties without impact-rated openings was significant and widespread. Wyndham-area homes that relied on traditional doors with standard hardware saw those doors blown in, warped, or completely compromised. Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced the lesson again, delivering tropical storm to Category 1 force winds across much of Broward County, toppling trees, and testing every home's envelope integrity.

For the approximately 25% of Wyndham homes built before 1994, the urgency is even greater. Pre-code construction standards allowed for door systems that simply were not designed with hurricane survival in mind. If your home falls into that category, upgrading to certified impact doors is one of the most consequential improvements you can make to protect your family and your property when the next significant storm tracks through South Florida.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Broward County
section_code.body:
Wyndham falls within Broward County's jurisdiction, which means all door replacements and new installations must comply with the Florida Building Code (FBC) — one of the most stringent residential construction codes in the United States. While Wyndham is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), which applies primarily to Miami-Dade and Broward coastal areas, the community still falls within a 140 mph wind zone designation. This means that any impact door installed in a Wyndham home must be rated to withstand large missile impact testing and cyclic wind pressure loads as defined under ASTM and FBC standards.

All impact door products installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing in Wyndham carry the required Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or Florida Product Approval (FPA) documentation. These certifications confirm that the door units have been independently tested and verified to meet the performance requirements for your specific wind zone. Products without these certifications cannot legally be installed in Broward County homes and will not pass inspection. This distinction matters greatly when you're comparing quotes — a lower bid that includes non-certified products is not just a code violation, it's a liability.

Permits for impact door installations in Wyndham are processed through the Broward County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit application process on your behalf, submitting product approval documentation, installation drawings, and any required engineering details. Once work is completed, we coordinate the required building inspection to ensure your installation is officially closed out and on record. This documentation is critical not only for code compliance but also for insurance purposes and for protecting your home's resale value in the competitive Wyndham real estate market.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Protection Built for Wyndham Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most respected impact door brands in the South Florida market: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both manufacturers engineer their products specifically for Florida's extreme climate conditions, and both carry the product approvals required for installation in Broward County's 140 mph wind zone. When you choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not getting a generic big-box store door — you're getting a purpose-built, hurricane-rated system designed to perform when it matters most.

ESW impact entry doors and sliding glass doors are engineered with heavy-duty aluminum frames, laminated impact glass, and multi-point locking systems that engage at multiple points along the door frame simultaneously. This multi-point design dramatically increases the door's resistance to forced entry as well as wind-driven pressure. ESW's sliding glass door systems are particularly popular in Wyndham Estates homes, where large rear openings and patio access points are common architectural features that require robust protection without sacrificing aesthetic appeal. ESW products are available in a variety of finishes, hardware options, and glass configurations to complement the elevated design standards of upper-middle-income Wyndham homes.

CWI impact doors offer exceptional performance in both entry door and French door applications. CWI's reinforced frame systems and impact-laminated glass panels meet all Florida Building Code requirements for the 33076 area, and their product line includes options suited to the Mediterranean and contemporary architectural styles common throughout Wyndham Estates. French doors from CWI are an especially popular upgrade for Wyndham homeowners looking to replace traditional outswing or inswing doors with systems that combine elegance and hurricane-rated protection. Every ESW and CWI product we install comes backed by manufacturer warranties, and our in-house installation guarantee ensures the workmanship is covered as well.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Wyndham — and Why They're Worth Every Dollar
section_cost.body:
For a Wyndham home with an average value of approximately $480,000, investing in certified impact doors is one of the highest-return improvements available. The cost of impact door installation varies based on the number of openings, door type, and configuration — entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors each carry different price points based on size and complexity. As a general benchmark, homeowners in the Wyndham area can expect single impact entry door replacements to start in the mid-hundreds to low thousands, while larger sliding glass door systems and multi-panel French door configurations will represent a greater investment. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides free, no-pressure estimates so you know exactly what to expect before any work begins.

To make hurricane protection accessible for every Wyndham homeowner, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month, with $0 down programs available for qualified applicants. This means you don't have to wait, save, or delay your family's protection to get the impact doors your home needs. Spreading the cost over time while immediately benefiting from the protection, energy efficiency, and insurance savings that impact doors provide makes the financial decision straightforward for most Wyndham households.

The return on investment extends well beyond storm protection. Impact doors improve your home's energy efficiency by reducing air infiltration and solar heat gain — particularly meaningful in South Florida's intense summer heat. They also enhance security against forced entry, reduce outside noise, and meaningfully contribute to your home's appraised value. In the competitive Wyndham real estate market, homes with fully permitted, certified impact openings consistently attract stronger buyer interest and appraise at higher values than comparable properties without this upgrade.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Wyndham Homeowners Who Install Impact Doors
section_insurance.body:
One of the most immediate and tangible financial benefits of installing impact doors in your Wyndham home is the reduction in homeowners insurance premiums. Florida's insurance market has experienced significant upheaval in recent years, with many carriers tightening underwriting standards and raising rates throughout Broward County. However, certified impact-rated openings — including impact doors — are one of the most recognized and rewarded upgrades in the wind mitigation process. A completed wind mitigation inspection that documents your impact-rated doors and windows can result in meaningful annual premium discounts, often enough to make the upgrade cost-neutral within a few years.

Wind mitigation credits in Florida are governed by the Florida Department of Financial Services and apply across all major homeowners insurance carriers operating in the state. When a licensed wind mitigation inspector verifies that your home's openings are protected by certified impact-rated products, your insurer is required to apply the appropriate credits to your policy. For Wyndham homeowners in the 140 mph wind zone, these credits can be substantial — many policyholders report wind mitigation savings ranging from hundreds to over a thousand dollars annually depending on their coverage levels and insurer.

Beyond wind mitigation credits, some carriers offer additional discounts for homes where all openings — including entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors — are fully protected. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can connect you with a licensed wind mitigation inspector after your installation is complete, and we provide all necessary product documentation, NOA numbers, and installation records to support your claim. In an insurance environment where Wyndham homeowners are seeing rates climb year over year, locking in these credits through a fully certified impact door installation is a proactive and financially sound strategy.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Wyndham if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Wyndham sits about 12 miles from the coast, but Broward County's 140 mph wind zone designation applies throughout the area — including the 33076 ZIP code. Major hurricanes like Wilma in 2005 and Irma in 2017 demonstrated that wind and debris damage extends far inland. Your entry points remain vulnerable regardless of coastal proximity, and Florida Building Code requires hurricane-rated products for all door replacements in Wyndham.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Wyndham, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Costs vary based on door type, size, and the number of openings. Impact entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors each have different price points. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free estimates tailored to your Wyndham home. We also offer financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners, making it easy to upgrade without waiting or depleting savings.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors with impact doors in Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes. All impact door installations in Wyndham require a permit through the Broward County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process for you — we prepare and submit all required documentation, including Florida Product Approval numbers and installation details, and we coordinate the final building inspection. You don't have to navigate the county permit office on your own.

faqs.q: How long does an impact door installation take in Wyndham?
faqs.a: Most residential impact door installations in Wyndham are completed in one to two days, depending on the number of openings and the complexity of the project. The permitting process typically takes one to three weeks prior to installation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's in-house crews work efficiently to minimize disruption to your household, and we coordinate all required inspections to ensure a smooth, fully closed-out permit before we consider the job complete.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors lower my homeowners insurance in Wyndham?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. A wind mitigation inspection completed after your impact door installation documents your protected openings for your insurer. Florida law requires carriers to apply wind mitigation credits, which can reduce your premium by hundreds to over a thousand dollars annually depending on your policy and coverage levels. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all documentation needed to support your wind mitigation claim, and the long-term savings often offset a substantial portion of the installation cost.
